Journal.Title: Journal of colloid and interface science

Article.ArticleTitle: Polyoxometalates subnanowires-reinforced covalent organic framework nanosheets membranes for enhanced proton conductivity and mechanical property.

Abstract.AbstractText: Two-dimensional covalent organic framework nanosheets (CONs) as building blocks for proton exchange membrane (PEM) have unique advantages such as highly ordered nanochannels and precisely designed chemical structures while facing the challenge of insufficient mechanical strength due to weak interlayer interaction. In this work, using Ca-POM SNWs (Ca-Polyoxometalates Sub-nanowires) as binder and CONs (CON-2(SO3H)) as membrane building unit, a series of CON-2(SO3H)/SNWs composite membranes are constructed for improved proton conductivity and mechanical property. By the aid of the strong electrostatic interaction between the protonated oleylamine on the surface of Ca-POM SNWs and the sulfonic acid group of CON-2(SO3H), the CONs are induced to form densely ordered stacking structure, creating continuous proton channels and enhancing structural stability of the membrane in the hydrated state. Ultimately, the CON-2(SO3H)/SNWs-2 membrane attains a proton conductivity of 435.06 mS cm-1 at 80 °C with an activation energy of only 0.10 eV, a tensile strength of 31.88 MPa and exceptional stability retaining 93 % of its conductivity after harsh acid treatment. This study proposes a strategy for designing high-performance PEMs that exploits the strong electrostatic interaction between multifunctional one-dimensional nanomaterials and COFs.
